items.text:
DMS – Domain Training Documentation 
SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION TO DMS 
What is a DMS? 
A Dealer Management System (DMS) is an integrated software platform designed to manage and streamline the operations of automotive dealerships. It handles functions such as sales, service, inventory, customer relationship management (CRM), finance, and reporting. 
Users & Industry Importance 
DMS is widely used by OEMs ((Original Equipment Manufacturer), dealerships, service centres, technicians, and sales teams. It enhances efficiency, improves customer service, ensures real-time data flow, and strengthens coordination across the automotive ecosystem. By centralizing operations, it helps reduce manual errors and supports better decision-making. 
DMS in Cloud vs. On-Premises 
Cloud-based DMS: Offers scal

ps reduce manual errors and supports better decision-making. 
DMS in Cloud vs. On-Premises 
Cloud-based DMS: Offers scalability, remote access, lower IT overhead, automatic updates, and robust data backup. 
On-premises DMS: Offers greater control and customization but requires higher infrastructure investment and ongoing maintenance. 
Real-Time Example 
Maruti Suzuki, one of India’s leading automotive manufacturers, uses a DMS to automate its end-to-end dealership operations. This includes vehicle ordering, inventory management, customer service, billing, and after-sales support — enabling seamless coordination across its vast dealership network. 
 
SECTION 2: CORE MODULES IN DMS 
2.1 Vehicle Sales 
 
The Vehicle Sales module in the DMS streamlines the end-to-end sales process — from captu

S IN DMS 
2.1 Vehicle Sales 
 
The Vehicle Sales module in the DMS streamlines the end-to-end sales process — from capturing leads to final delivery and invoicing. It integrates with core business systems for seamless operations and better customer experience. 
        1.Vehicle Master Setup 
Maintains master data for all vehicle models and variants. 
Includes specifications, pricing, colour options, and availability. 
Forms the foundation for inventory tracking and quotation. 
 2. Lead Capture & Management 
Captures leads from multiple sources (walk-ins, web, call centres). 
Tracks lead status: new, contacted, qualified, or lost. 
Assigns lead to sales reps automatically or manually. 
Integrated with CRM for follow-ups and communication logs. 
 3. Quotation Generation 
Enables quick and a

or manually. 
Integrated with CRM for follow-ups and communication logs. 
 3. Quotation Generation 
Enables quick and accurate quote creation for selected vehicles. 
Pulls pricing and offer details from master data. 
Supports multiple versions of a quote and customer negotiation history. 
Includes discount handling and approval workflows. 
 4. Booking to Delivery Workflow 
Converts accepted quotes into bookings. 
Tracks the vehicle allocation from inventory. 
Manages PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection), documentation, and delivery dates. 
Real-time updates to sales and inventory teams. 
 5. Sales Invoice & RTO Registration 
Auto-generates sales invoices linked to booking details. 
Manages statutory requirements like road tax, insurance, and registration. 
Integrated workflow with RTO agents for

ls. 
Manages statutory requirements like road tax, insurance, and registration. 
Integrated workflow with RTO agents for registration and number plate issuance. 
 6. System Integrations 
CRM: For lead nurturing, campaign tracking, and customer lifecycle. 
Inventory: Real-time availability and stock management. 
Finance API: EMI calculators, loan approvals, and finance partner sync. 
Example Workflow: Booking a Swift 
Lead captured via website for Maruti Swift. 
Sales rep creates and sends quotation. 
Customer confirms and pays booking amount. 
Vehicle allocated from inventory, PDI completed. 
Invoice generated, RTO process initiated. 
Vehicle delivered to customer with documents. 
 
2.2 Service Management 
 
The Service Management module in the DMS enables end-to-end tracking of vehicle se

cuments. 
 
2.2 Service Management 
 
The Service Management module in the DMS enables end-to-end tracking of vehicle servicing operations — from job card creation to final invoicing. It ensures efficient technician workflows, parts tracking, and warranty processing. 
 
			 
1. Repair Order (RO) Creation 
 
Initiates the service process by creating a unique Repair Order (RO). 
Includes customer details, vehicle info, service type (scheduled, breakdown, body repair), and complaint description. 
Auto-checks for service history and warranty status. 
2. Job Card Lifecycle 
Tracks all stages: Opened → In Progress → →Booking Completed → Close RO. 
Job card captures customer concerns, technician notes, work performed, and timestamps. 
Links to Opcodes, labour, and parts details. 
3. Technician As

cerns, technician notes, work performed, and timestamps. 
Links to Opcodes, labour, and parts details. 
3. Technician Assignment 
Assigns jobs to technicians based on skills and availability. 
Enables load balancing across bays. 
Tracks technician productivity and job turnaround time. 
 4. Opcodes (Operation Codes) 
Standardized service operations like oil change, brake inspection, AC service, etc. 
Each OpCode includes estimated labor time, standard pricing, and parts linkage. 
Used for efficiency tracking and billing consistency. 
 5. Parts Request and Usage 
Enables service advisors or technicians to raise parts requests directly from the job card. 
Pulls stock availability from inventory in real-time. 
Tracks actual usage vs estimate, including returns and shortages. 
 6. Warranty Clai

ility from inventory in real-time. 
Tracks actual usage vs estimate, including returns and shortages. 
 6. Warranty Claims 
Manages service jobs under OEM warranty. 
Automatically checks vehicle warranty status. 
Prepares claim documents, integrates with OEM claim portals (if applicable). 
Tracks claim approval and reimbursement status. 
 7. Payment and Invoicing 
Consolidates labor, parts, taxes, and discounts into a final invoice. 
Supports multiple payment modes: cash, card, digital, loyalty points. 
Applies free service coupons or AMC discounts as applicable. 
 8. RO Dashboard Grids 
Displays RO status: open, in progress, ready, closed. 
Includes filters by technician, bay, customer, job type, and date. 
Helps service managers monitor bottlenecks and daily throughput. 
 Example Workflo

, bay, customer, job type, and date. 
Helps service managers monitor bottlenecks and daily throughput. 
 Example Workflow: Service Visit for Engine Noise 
RO Created for a vehicle reporting engine noise. 
Job Card Opened with customer complaint and warranty check. 
OpCodes Added for engine diagnostics and test run. 
Technician Assigned and parts requested from store. 
Work completed, QC done, and final invoice generated. 
Customer billed; job card closed, and RO archived. 
2.3 Parts Management 
The Parts Management module plays a critical role in ensuring the availability, tracking, and ordering of spare parts within an automotive dealership. It integrates with other modules like Service, Inventory, and Supplier Systems (e.g., SAP) to streamline operations and prevent stockouts or overstoc

les like Service, Inventory, and Supplier Systems (e.g., SAP) to streamline operations and prevent stockouts or overstocking. 
Parts Master Setup 
Acts as the central repository for all spare parts information. 
Stores standardized data such as: 
Part Number 
Description 
Unit of Measure 
Manufacturer/Supplier 
Cost & Pricing (Retail, Wholesale) 
Applicable Vehicle Models 
Ensures data consistency across purchase, sales, and service modules. 
Helps in cross-referencing parts and managing equivalent/substitute items. 
 
Parts Inward / Outward 
Parts Inward: 
Records the receipt of parts from suppliers or warehouses. 
Includes details like delivery note, invoice number, batch number, and received quantity. 
Supports quality check (QC) and marking of damaged/returnable items. 
Parts Outward:

ch number, and received quantity. 
Supports quality check (QC) and marking of damaged/returnable items. 
Parts Outward: 
Issues parts to various departments: 
Workshop Jobs (linked to job cards) 
Retail Sales to customers 
Inter-branch transfers 
Tracks parts issued vs. stock and supports real-time updates. 
Bin Location Mapping 
Assigns specific bin locations to each part within the store/warehouse. 
Aids in faster retrieval and accurate inventory count. 
Minimizes misplacement and improves inventory accuracy. 
Often includes: 
Bin No. 
Rack/Shelf structure 
Warehouse/Store ID 
 Inventory Management 
Continuously monitors stock levels, part movement, and reorder points. 
Key Features: 
FIFO/LIFO costing 
Cycle counting 
Stock adjustments (e.g., damage, return) 
Aging analysis for slow-mov

Key Features: 
FIFO/LIFO costing 
Cycle counting 
Stock adjustments (e.g., damage, return) 
Aging analysis for slow-moving items 
Provides stock visibility across multiple locations/branches. 
Ordering & Supplier Integration 
Automatically generates purchase orders based on: 
Stock thresholds 
Demand forecasting 
Historical consumption patterns 
Integration with external ERP systems like SAP enables: 
Real-time supplier communication 
Electronic order placement and tracking 
Invoice matching and reconciliation 
 
Example: Low Stock Alert & Ordering via SAP 
When the stock of a fast-moving part (e.g., brake pads) falls below the minimum threshold: 
DMS triggers a low stock alert. 
Auto-generates a purchase order. 
Sends order to SAP for processing and vendor coordination. 
Once received, p

k alert. 
Auto-generates a purchase order. 
Sends order to SAP for processing and vendor coordination. 
Once received, part inward entry updates inventory and bin mapping. 
 
2.4 Customer Management 
The Customer Management module in a DMS plays a pivotal role in centralizing and organizing customer-related data and interactions. It integrates with Vehicle Sales, Service, and CRM systems to deliver personalized experiences, improve service quality, and drive customer retention through targeted campaigns and loyalty programs. 
Customer master 
Serves as the central repository for all customer information, including: 
Name, contact details, and address. 
ID proofs (PAN, Aadhaar, etc.) 
Communication preferences (SMS, email, phone). 
Linked vehicles and service history. 
 
Complaint registrat

haar, etc.) 
Communication preferences (SMS, email, phone). 
Linked vehicles and service history. 
 
Complaint registration & tracking 
Allows staff to log, manage, and resolve customer complaints across any touchpoint—sales, service, parts, or billing. 
Unique complaint ID for tracking. 
SLA monitoring and auto-escalations. 
Complaint analytics (frequent issues, repeat complaints). 
 
Feedback collection 
Enables systematic capture of customer feedback after major touchpoints like vehicle delivery or service visit. 
Used for NPS (Net Promoter Score) and CSI (Customer Satisfaction Index). 
Feedback triggers corrective actions or reward programs. 
Campaign management 
Used by CRM and Marketing teams to run targeted promotional or awareness campaigns. 
Campaign Types: 
New model launch promo

by CRM and Marketing teams to run targeted promotional or awareness campaigns. 
Campaign Types: 
New model launch promotions. 
Service reminders (due for periodic maintenance). 
AMC (Annual Maintenance Contract) renewal drives. 
Festival offers or exchange bonuses. 
 
Loyalty programs 
Designed to reward repeat customers and build long-term relationships. 
Encourages customer retention and repeat business. 
Enhances brand image and customer advocacy. 
 
2.5 Finance & Accounting 
The Finance & Accounting module ensures seamless financial operations within the dealership by integrating with ERPs and automating key workflows related to billing, commissions, credit, and ledgers. 
ERP Integration 
Bi-directional sync with external ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle). 
Enables accurate posting of i

ers. 
ERP Integration 
Bi-directional sync with external ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle). 
Enables accurate posting of invoices, payments, and journal entries. 
General Ledger, AP/AR 
Auto-posting to General Ledger for: 
Vehicle sales 
Workshop jobs 
Parts transactions 
Tracks: 
Accounts Receivable: Customer dues, follow-up status. 
Accounts Payable: Vendor invoices, payment schedules. 
Commission Calculation 
Sales incentive rules based on vehicle type, target achievement, margin, etc. 
Generates reports for monthly or ad-hoc incentive payments. 
Includes approval workflows and exception handling. 
Credit Limit Validation 
Validates credit customers against predefined limits during billing. 
Blocks or warns on exceeding limit. 
Useful for fleet customers and B2B clients. 
 
2.6 Insurance

mits during billing. 
Blocks or warns on exceeding limit. 
Useful for fleet customers and B2B clients. 
 
2.6 Insurance & Extended Warranty 
This module manages insurance and extended warranty services offered during or after vehicle sales. 
Insurance Quote Generation 
Integrated with insurance partners (e.g., Bajaj Allianz, ICICI Lombard). 
Fetches real-time quotes based on vehicle model and customer profile. 
Allows selection and printing of quote during vehicle booking. 
 Insurance Renewal 
Alerts for upcoming renewals. 
Enables policy renewal directly from DMS or via SMS/email campaigns. 
Policy Mapping with Vehicle 
Links insurance policy to vehicle in the customer profile. 
Ensures claims or renewals can be tracked historically. 
Example 
A sales executive generates a Bajaj Allianz i

rofile. 
Ensures claims or renewals can be tracked historically. 
Example 
A sales executive generates a Bajaj Allianz insurance quote for a Maruti Swift directly within the DMS during booking. Once the customer approves, policy details are stored and linked with the vehicle record. 
 
2.7 CRM & Lead Management 
This module enhances customer engagement through lifecycle tracking, campaign effectiveness, and lead nurturing. 
Lead Capture 
Captures lead from: 
OEM portals 
Walk-ins 
Websites 
Events 
Referrals 
OEM CRM Integration 
Syncs leads and campaigns with OEM CRM (e.g., Hyundai MOBIS, Honda CRM). 
Tracks DMS-originated leads vs. OEM-assigned leads. 
Follow-up & Closure 
Automated and manual follow-ups with status tracking. 
Lead stages: New → Contacted → Qualified → Lost/Won. 
Campaig

re 
Automated and manual follow-ups with status tracking. 
Lead stages: New → Contacted → Qualified → Lost/Won. 
Campaign Analytics 
Tracks ROI on campaigns. 
Filters by region, model, source, and outcome. 
 
2.8 Workshop Management 
Optimizes service bay utilization and technician efficiency. 
Bay Management 
Maps service bays with vehicle types. 
Auto-allocates bays based on job priority and technician load. 
Service Appointment Booking 
Customer can schedule visits via call, website, or mobile app. 
Assigns time slots based on estimated job duration. 
Technician Time Tracking 
Captures time spent on each job card. 
Supports incentive calculation and productivity metrics. 
Live Vehicle Tracking 
Real-time job status visible to front desk and customers. 
Integration with token systems or

s. 
Live Vehicle Tracking 
Real-time job status visible to front desk and customers. 
Integration with token systems or dashboards in waiting areas. 
 
Ensures digital handling of critical documents. 
Digital Documentation 
Upload and manage: 
RTO documents 
KYC forms 
Insurance scans 
Vehicle RC & invoices 
Secure Storage & Retrieval 
Role-based access control. 
Searchable archive with date/version filters. 
 
2.10 Reporting & Analytics 
Offers actionable insights to improve business decisions. 
 Standard Reports 
Predefined reports like: 
Daily Sales Report (DSR) 
Workshop Load Report 
Parts Consumption Report 
 Custom Dashboards 
Technician productivity, parts aging, lead conversion, etc. 
Configurable widgets and KPIs. 
Power BI/Tableau Integration 
Export data to external BI tools for

ead conversion, etc. 
Configurable widgets and KPIs. 
Power BI/Tableau Integration 
Export data to external BI tools for deeper analytics. 
Scheduled refreshes for executive dashboards. 
 
 SECTION 3: ADVANCED FUNCTIONAL MODULES 
3.1 Warranty Claims & Returns 
The Warranty Claims & Returns module manages all post-service warranty operations between the dealership and the OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer). It ensures proper tracking, documentation, and reimbursement of service claims and returned parts. 
Claim Process 
Tracks service operations (repairs, part replacements) that are covered under warranty based on the job card and vehicle warranty period. 
Auto-generates digital warranty claim forms based on the service job card, capturing: 
Labor details (tasks performed, time, cost) 
P

s digital warranty claim forms based on the service job card, capturing: 
Labor details (tasks performed, time, cost) 
Parts used (part numbers, quantities, pricing) 
Ensures proper tagging of claims with vehicle VIN, warranty policy type, and customer details for accurate traceability. 
 
Return & Approval Workflow 
Manages the return process for parts that need to be sent back to the OEM or warehouse for inspection. 
Automates the creation of part return requests with reference to claim numbers. 
Routes requests through a configurable approval chain (Service Manager → Warranty Auditor → Finance/OEM Team). 
Generates system-led credit note entries upon approval for financial reconciliation. 
              OEM Integration 
Integrates with OEM systems/portals (via API/web services) for real

cial reconciliation. 
              OEM Integration 
Integrates with OEM systems/portals (via API/web services) for real-time: 
Claim submission 
Documentation upload (photos, invoices, job cards) 
Claim tracking (status updates like "Under Review", "Approved", "Rejected") 
Receives reimbursement data directly from OEM, updating the dealership’s financials. 
Tracks reimbursement timelines for better follow-up and financial forecasting. 
Business Impact: Enhances operational transparency, reduces reimbursement delays, minimizes rejected claims due to data errors, and improves OEM-dealer coordination. 
 
3.2 Pre-owned Vehicle Module 
The Pre-owned Vehicle module handles the complete lifecycle of used vehicle transactions—from acquisition and valuation to refurbishment and resale. 
Vehicle Ev

complete lifecycle of used vehicle transactions—from acquisition and valuation to refurbishment and resale. 
Vehicle Evaluation 
A structured on-road and off-road inspection is performed by evaluators using a digital checklist. 
Evaluation includes: 
Visual inspection: Dents, scratches, paintwork, interiors. 
Mechanical inspection: Engine, brakes, tires, suspension, battery, etc. 
Mileage and service history 
Based on the checklist, an estimated resale value is calculated using internal pricing algorithms or integration with external valuation tools. 
 Procurement 
Captures detailed seller information: name, ID proof, vehicle documents (RC, insurance, etc.) 
Facilitates digital agreement creation, capturing purchase price and terms. 
System generates an inward GRN (Goods Receipt Note), of

digital agreement creation, capturing purchase price and terms. 
System generates an inward GRN (Goods Receipt Note), officially adding the vehicle to the used stock inventory. 
Option to initiate RTO name transfer and insurance verification. 
Refurbishing 
Generates job cards for any repairs or cosmetic upgrades before resale. 
Assigns internal service bays or third-party vendors to complete refurbishing. 
Tracks refurbishment cost (parts, labor, vendor charges) and links it to the vehicle for margin analysis upon sale. 
Supports image capture before and after refurbishing for transparency. 
Sale Process 
Used cars are treated as a separate inventory category with custom workflows. 
Supports: 
Customer enquiry management 
Test drive scheduling 
Quotation generation and approval 
Used car

workflows. 
Supports: 
Customer enquiry management 
Test drive scheduling 
Quotation generation and approval 
Used car invoicing, insurance transfer, and extended warranty addition (if applicable) 
Captures trade-in/exchange scenarios where the customer buys a new vehicle in exchange for their used one. 
Business Impact: Enables a profitable used car business with structured evaluation, cost control on refurbishing, and transparent resale processes, driving revenue growth. 
 
3.3 Accessories Management 
The Accessories Management module enables dealerships to increase revenue by offering and managing vehicle accessories during the booking, sales, and after-sales service phases. 
 Accessory Mapping 
Each vehicle model and variant is mapped to a list of compatible accessories. 
Prevents inc

phases. 
 Accessory Mapping 
Each vehicle model and variant is mapped to a list of compatible accessories. 
Prevents incompatible or incorrect accessory selection by enforcing compatibility rules. 
Enables sales advisors to offer model-specific accessories (e.g., alloy wheels for Model X, infotainment kits for Model Y). 
Supports grouping of accessories into "packs" (e.g., Sport Pack, Comfort Pack). 
 Stock & Ordering Flow 
Maintains real-time stock of accessories in the inventory. 
Tracks inward and outward movement of accessories through barcode/RFID scanning. 
Automatically raises purchase orders to vendors when stock falls below the defined minimum threshold. 
Manages bin-wise storage locations, part numbers, and reorder levels. 
Supports multiple vendor sourcing and pricing comparison

bin-wise storage locations, part numbers, and reorder levels. 
Supports multiple vendor sourcing and pricing comparison. 
Integrated Billing 
Accessories can be bundled with: 
New vehicle sales: Accessories are added to the vehicle invoice with breakdown. 
Workshop visits: Accessories are offered during service, billed on the same invoice. 
Handles discounts, offers, and combo pricing during accessory sales. 
Ensures proper financial accounting, GST tagging, and reporting in the ERP/finance system. 
 Sales Enablement 
Accessories can be showcased via digital catalogues or touchscreen kiosks at the showroom. 
Suggestive selling: System can recommend accessories based on the vehicle model, past purchase behaviour, or current service type. 
Tracks accessory sales performance per executive or

he vehicle model, past purchase behaviour, or current service type. 
Tracks accessory sales performance per executive or department 
 
SECTION 4: INTEGRATIONS 
 
A modern DMS integrates with multiple external systems to streamline business workflows, eliminate manual efforts, and ensure real-time data exchange. These integrations cover OEMs, government systems, financial institutions, insurers, inventory systems, and third-party services. 
4.1 OEM Integration 
Purpose: OEM integration connects a DMS (used by automotive dealerships) with the vehicle manufacturer's backend systems to enable real-time communication, data synchronization, and automated processes. This ensures that the dealership operations align with manufacturer standards and remain up to date with vehicle, warranty, and part

that the dealership operations align with manufacturer standards and remain up to date with vehicle, warranty, and parts data. 
Vehicle dispatch updates 
 Fetch dispatch status and VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) info directly from the OEM system. 
Auto-update DMS stock when vehicle leaves OEM plant. 
Warranty Validation 
During service, automatically check the vehicle’s warranty eligibility using VIN and service date. 
Reduces errors and manual lookup. 
VIN-based info pulls 
When a vehicle is registered or serviced, fetch details like engine number, variant, model year from OEM databases using the VIN. 
API-based example 
 
4.2 Finance & Loan Provider Integration 
Purpose: 
Enable dealerships to offer instant, seamless loan options to customers by integrating with banks and NBFCs via

urpose: 
Enable dealerships to offer instant, seamless loan options to customers by integrating with banks and NBFCs via APIs. 
Features: 
Loan Eligibility Checks 
Using customer PAN, Aadhaar, income proof, etc., fetch instant eligibility decisions from partners like HDFC, ICICI, Axis Bank, Kotak. 
Loan Application Submission 
Submit a digital loan request form (DLF) directly to the financier’s system via API. 
Loan Sanction & Disbursement Status 
Track status updates (Approved, Under Review, Disbursed, Rejected). 
 4.3 RTO / Government API Integration 
 Purpose: 
Automate compliance and registration workflows with Regional Transport Office (RTO) and government portals. 
 Features: 
Vehicle Registration Workflow 
Automate RC generation, number plate assignment, tax payment, and insurance s

Features: 
Vehicle Registration Workflow 
Automate RC generation, number plate assignment, tax payment, and insurance sync. 
Vehicle Fitness Certificate Check 
Retrieve real-time fitness status for commercial vehicles. 
Emission Certificate Integration 
Pull emission test status/certificates using vehicle number or VIN. 
 4.4 Insurance Partner Integration 
 Purpose: 
Provide customers with instant insurance quotes, policy comparisons, and digital policy issuance during vehicle sale. 
 Features: 
Real-time Premium Quote API 
Fetch quotes based on customer, vehicle type, IDV, policy type (Comprehensive/Third-party). 
Comparison Engine 
Compare quotes across providers (Bajaj Allianz, ICICI Lombard, HDFC Ergo, etc.). 
Digital Policy Issuance 
Generate PDF policy documents with e-signature and

j Allianz, ICICI Lombard, HDFC Ergo, etc.). 
Digital Policy Issuance 
Generate PDF policy documents with e-signature and share via email/WhatsApp. 
 
 4.5 Inventory System Integration (e.g., SAP, Oracle) 
                    Purpose: 
Connect DMS with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, MS Dynamics) for inventory and procurement workflows. 
 Features: 
Stock Synchronization 
Real-time update of spare parts and vehicle stock between SAP and DMS. 
Purchase Order (PO) Flow 
Auto-create PO from DMS, sync with ERP for approval, and push back PO number/status. 
Goods Receipt Note (GRN) 
When parts/vehicles arrive, DMS generates GRN and syncs with ERP. 
 
4.6 Third-party Tool Integration 
Purpose: 
Enhance customer engagement, communication, and feedback using third-party services. 
Key Integrations: 
SMS/

Purpose: 
Enhance customer engagement, communication, and feedback using third-party services. 
Key Integrations: 
SMS/Email Engines 
Integrate with vendors like Exotel, Twilio, Netcore, Msg91. 
Used for: 
OTPs 
Service Reminders 
Promotional Campaigns 
 WhatsApp Integration 
Via WhatsApp Business API or Twilio, send: 
Appointment confirmations 
Insurance reminders 
Invoice copies 
Service tracking 
 Service Feedback Integration 
Integrate with platforms like SurveyMonkey, Google Forms, or Netcore Feedback. 
Automatically send feedback forms post-service or vehicle delivery.

What Are User Roles in DMS? 
User roles define

Permissions & approval matrix

What Are User Roles in DMS? 
User roles define the responsibilities and access levels of different users within the DMS. These roles ensure data security, process control, and accountability by restricting system access to only what's necessary for each user. 
Common Role Types in DMS
